chainguard.capabilities.Capability.description:
Capability is an enumeration of the Chainguard IAM capabilities.

 - CAP_TENANT_VULN_REPORTS_LIST: TODO(colin): deprecate this cap once the legacy endpoint is removed
 - CAP_REPO_BLOBS_GET: repo.blobs.get is a capability required to pull container images.
We explicitly define this to distinguish roles that allow browsing/viewing
metadata associated with images vs pulling the image itself.
 - CAP_APK_BLOBS_GET: apk.blobs.get is a capability required to pull APKs.
We explicitly define this to distinguish roles that allow browsing/viewing
metadata associated with APKs vs pulling the APK itself.
 - CAP_BUILD_REPORT_LIST: Custom Image Build
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_ENTITLEMENTS_CREATE: Global libraries capabilities
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_PYTHON_LIST: Ecosystems specific capabilities
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_CACHE_INVALIDATE: Cache invalidation capability for the libraries cache (covers all ecosystems).
Granted to org owners and account admins; not to regular developer members.
 - CAP_LIBRARIES_REBUILDER_REQUESTS_CREATE: Rebuilder API capabilities
 - CAP_REGISTRY_ENTITLEMENTS_LIST: Registry Entitlements
 - CAP_REGISTRY_SETTINGS_LIST: Registry Settings
 - CAP_REGISTRY_DEPLOYMENTS_CREATE: Registry Deployments
 - CAP_POLICY_GATES_POLICY_CREATE: Policy Gates
 - CAP_TERMS_ACCEPT: Terms
 - CAP_GULFSTREAM: This is orthogonal enough that we should leave
it somewhat separate, so add new capabilities above.
TODO(mattmoor): Think about whether we can encode specific
controller capabilities into our access control here?
e.g. could each logical controller/webhook be its own
capability?
 - CAP_GUARDENER_DFC_CONVERT: Guardener
 - CAP_MCP_TOOL_CALL: MCP tool calls

chainguard.platform.advisory.Reference.T.description:
- UNSPECIFIED: The "unset" value.
 - ADVISORY: Published security advisory for the vulnerability.
 - ARTICLE: Article or blog post describing the vulnerability.
 - DETECTION: Scanner or tool that allows detection in prod environments.
 - DISCUSSION: Social media discussion about vuln.
 - REPORT: Report on vuln.
 - FIX: Source code browser link to fix.
 - INTRODUCED: Source code browser link to introduction of vuln.
 - PACKAGE: Home web page for package.
 - EVIDENCE: Demonstration of validity of vuln claim.
 - WEB: Web page of unspecified kind.

chainguard.platform.apk.APKPackage.description:
APKPackage is an APK package and its metadata.

An APK can be downloaded by requesting
https://apk.cgr.dev/{group}/{arch}/{pkgname}-{pkgver}.apk
with suitable credentials.

The fields 10+ are the fields that make up the APKINDEX format
from here: https://wiki.alpinelinux.org/wiki/Apk_spec#APKINDEX_Format

ecdhPublicKey.description:
When establishing a headless session, the client must first provide
their ECDH public key. The server will then generate an ephemeral
ECHD keypair and use the client's public key to establish a symmetric
key.

This symmetric key will be used to encrypt the idtoken and stored the
encrypted idtoken in the database for a short period, as well as
returning it to the client.

After the idtoken is encrypted, the server will discard both the shared
symmetric key and the ephemeral ECDH private key. The server ECDH will
be sent back to the client.

The client, with their private key, can use this public key to
establish the same symmetric key, which can be used to decrypt
the idtoken.

echd_public_key is the client's ECDH public key.

service_principal is the name of the Chainguard service that is

static.description:
static is equivalent to literal, but instead of discovering the
IdP's verification keys we verify the identity using pre-registered
verification keys.  This is intended for use with identities from
providers without an "online" issuer (network accessible to our IdP),
such as a KinD/minikube cluster.

the identity will expire, with a maximum lifetime of 30d.

 - ORG_KIND_STARTER: Free catalog starter organizations.
 - ORG_KIND_CUSTOMER: Paid customer organizations.
 - ORG_KIND_DEV: Personal developer organizations.
 - ORG_KIND_INFRA: Organizations that are used for infrastructure or automation.

chainguard.platform.iam.OrgStatus.description:
OrgStatus is the provisioning status of an organization.

 - ORG_STATUS_INITIALIZING: The organization is awaiting provisioning setup by a reconciler.
 - ORG_STATUS_READY: The organization is fully provisioned and ready to use.

OrgKind is the kind of organization the group belongs to.

 - ORG_KIND_STARTER: Free catalog starter organizations.

chainguard.platform.iam.v2beta1.ServiceP.description:
ServicePrincipal represents a Chainguard service that can assume an identity.

 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_UNSPECIFIED: Unknown/unspecified service principal.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_COSIGNED: Cosigned service for image signing.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_INGESTER: Ingester service for data ingestion.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_CATALOG_SYNCER: Catalog syncer service for synchronizing image catalogs.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_APKO_BUILDER: APKO builder service for building images.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_ENTITLEMENT_SYNCER: Entitlement syncer service for synchronizing entitlements.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_TENANT_SCANNER: Tenant scanner service for scanning tenant resources.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_SEDIMENTOLOGY: Sedimentology service for data analysis.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_SKILLUP: Skillup service for fixing repository skill violations.
 - SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_MATERIALIZER: Materializer service for materializing solutions from problem statements.

chainguard.platform.vulnerabilities.v2be.description:
The type of false positive this vulnerability was determined to be.

 - TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: The unset false positive type. This value is typically not valid for interactions
with the API.
 - TYPE_VULNERABILITY_RECORD_ANALYSIS_CONTESTED: Indicates that the distro maintainers view the vulnerability record itself
to be describing a behavior that is not a security concern or that
misattributes security fault to the software in the distro package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_COMPONENT_VULNERABILITY_MISMATCH: Indicates that the component referred to by the vulnerability record is not
the component found in the distribution package. (For example, perhaps a
vulnerability scanner found a vulnerability for a package with the same name,
but for a different language ecosystem.)

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"component_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_VERSION_NOT_USED: Indicates that the vulnerability was correctly matched to the component, except
that the version(s) of the component referred to by the vulnerability record have
never been present in a release of the distribution package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PACKAGE: Indicates that the vulnerable code (e.g. a particular function) may have been
available for use or retrieved during the package build process but ultimately
was not included in the distro package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_present" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_NOT_IN_EXECUTION_PATH: Indicates that the vulnerable code (e.g. a particular function) is present in
the package, but it is impossible for this code to be executed in the package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_not_in_execute_path" justification.
 - TYPE_VULNERABLE_CODE_CANNOT_BE_CONTROLLED_BY_ADVERSARY: Indicates that the vulnerable code is present and able to be executed, but
not in a way that can be exploited by an adversary.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"vulnerable_code_cannot_be_controlled_by_adversary" justification.
 - TYPE_INLINE_MITIGATIONS_EXIST: Indicates that the vulnerable code is present and able to be exploited by an
adversary, but that the vulnerability is mitigated by other code in the package.

VEX compatibility note: this type should be mapped to the
"inline_mitigations_already_exist" justification.

parameters.description:
One or more group scopes to restrict the returned token to.
If scope and scopes are both provided, the union of their values is
considered, after deduplication.

The returned token will include role bindings for the requested scopes,
if any exist, that are either granted directly at that scope or inherited from
its ancestors. That is, a role binding granted at a "lower" scope in the ancestry
applies to all descendants of that scope.

For example, given a role binding on group `foo` with id `foo/rb-id-1`
and a role binding on group `foo/bar` with id `foo/bar/rb-id-2`:
 - given scopes = [foo, foo/bar] => {foo: [foo/rb-id-1], foo/bar: [foo/bar/rb-id-2]}
 - given scopes = [foo/bar] => {foo/bar: [foo/rb-id-1, foo/bar/rb-id-2]}
parameters.name: scopes

parameters.description:
event_statuses filters by event review status (e.g., PENDING, REQUEST_CHANGES)
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event with any of the provided
statuses will be returned. This is useful for review workflows where users need to see
only events requiring action.
parameters.name: eventStatuses

parameters.description:
event_author filters by event author email
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event created by the specified
author will be returned. This enables users to find their own events or events from
specific team members.
parameters.name: eventAuthor

parameters.description:
event_reviewer filters by event reviewer email
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event reviewed by the specified
author will be returned. This enables users to find their own events or events from
specific team members.
parameters.name: eventReviewer

parameters.description:
event_types filters by event type
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event of any of the provided
types will be returned.

 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_UNSPECIFIED: Default/unspecified
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_DETECTION: Detection event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_TRUE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: True positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIXED: Fixed event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FALSE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: False positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_ANALYSIS_NOT_PLANNED: Analysis not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIX_NOT_PLANNED: Fix not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PENDING_UPSTREAM_FIX: Pending upstream fix
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PATCHED: Patched event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_COMPONENT_LOCATION_CHANGED: Component location changed event
parameters.name: eventTypes

parameters.description:
order_by specifies the sort order for results
Default: ID_ASC
This field affects the order of advisories returned, which is particularly useful
for pagination and review workflows.

 - ORDER_BY_UNSPECIFIED: Default sort order
 - ID_ASC: Sort by ID in ascending order
 - ID_DESC: Sort by ID in descending order
 - CREATED_AT_ASC: Sort by creation time in ascending order (oldest first)
 - CREATED_AT_DESC: Sort by creation time in descending order (newest first)
 - ALIASES_ASC: Sort by aliases to group related advisories in ascending order
 - ALIASES_DESC: Sort by aliases to group related advisories in descending order
 - UPDATED_AT_ASC: Sort by delete time in ascending order (oldest first)
 - UPDATED_AT_DESC: Sort by update time in descending order (newest first)
 - DELETED_AT_ASC: Sort by delete time in ascending order (oldest first)
 - DELETED_AT_DESC: Sort by delete time in descending order (newest first)
parameters.name: orderBy

parameters.description:
exclude_event_author filters out advisories where any event was created by the specified author
When specified, advisories containing events by this author will be excluded from results.
This is useful for review workflows where users want to see all pending events except their own,
enabling them to review others' work without seeing their own submissions.
parameters.name: excludeEventAuthor

parameters.description:
latest_event_type filters out advisories where the _most recent approved event_ is
of the given type. This is useful to find advisories which are currently detected,
fixed, patched, etc.

 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_UNSPECIFIED: Default/unspecified
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_DETECTION: Detection event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_TRUE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: True positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIXED: Fixed event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FALSE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: False positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_ANALYSIS_NOT_PLANNED: Analysis not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIX_NOT_PLANNED: Fix not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PENDING_UPSTREAM_FIX: Pending upstream fix
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PATCHED: Patched event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_COMPONENT_LOCATION_CHANGED: Component location changed event
parameters.name: latestEventType

parameters.description:
List advisories that match the list of event review states (e.g., PENDING, REQUEST_CHANGES).
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event with any of the provided
statuses will be returned. This is useful for review workflows where users need to see
only events requiring action.

 - REVIEW_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unknown/unspecified review status. This is not a valid status for an event.
 - REVIEW_STATE_PENDING: The event is pending review.
 - REVIEW_STATE_APPROVED: The event has been approved. This is a terminal state.
 - REVIEW_STATE_REQUEST_CHANGES: Changes have been requested by a reviewer.
 - REVIEW_STATE_REJECTED: The event has been rejected. This is a terminal state.
parameters.name: eventReviewStates

parameters.description:
Filters by event author email
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event created by the specified
author will be returned. This enables users to find their own events or events from
specific team members.
parameters.name: eventAuthors

parameters.description:
exclude_event_author filters out advisories where any event was created by the specified author
When specified, advisories containing events by this author will be excluded from results.
This is useful for review workflows where users want to see all pending events except their own,
enabling them to review others' work without seeing their own submissions.
parameters.name: excludeEventAuthors

parameters.description:
event_reviewer filters by event reviewer email
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event reviewed by the specified
author will be returned. This enables users to find their own events or events from
specific team members.
parameters.name: eventReviewers

parameters.description:
event_types filters by event type
When specified, only advisories containing at least one event of any of the provided
types will be returned.

 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_UNSPECIFIED: Default/unspecified
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_DETECTION: Detection event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_TRUE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: True positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIXED: Fixed event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FALSE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: False positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_ANALYSIS_NOT_PLANNED: Analysis not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIX_NOT_PLANNED: Fix not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PENDING_UPSTREAM_FIX: Pending upstream fix
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PATCHED: Patched event
parameters.name: eventTypes

parameters.description:
latest_event_type filters out advisories where the _most recent approved event_ is
of the given type. This is useful to find advisories which are currently detected,
fixed, patched, etc.

 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_UNSPECIFIED: Default/unspecified
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_DETECTION: Detection event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_TRUE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: True positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIXED: Fixed event
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FALSE_POSITIVE_DETERMINATION: False positive determination
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_ANALYSIS_NOT_PLANNED: Analysis not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_FIX_NOT_PLANNED: Fix not planned
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PENDING_UPSTREAM_FIX: Pending upstream fix
 - EVENT_TYPE_FILTER_PATCHED: Patched event
parameters.name: latestEventType

parameters.description:
Maximum number of results to return per page.
Default: 50, Maximum: 200.
parameters.name: pageSize

parameters.description:
Page token from a previous List response for pagination.
Opaque token with 3-day expiration.
parameters.name: pageToken

parameters.description:
Order results by field. Format: "field [asc|desc]"
Default: "uid asc"
Note: Changing order_by between pages invalidates the page token.
parameters.name: orderBy

parameters.description:
Number of results to skip before returning.
Used for random-access pagination (jumping to arbitrary pages).
Can be combined with page_token to skip from cursor position.
Must be non-negative.
parameters.name: skip

parameters.description:
Free-text search query. Performs case-insensitive substring matching across
artifact_name, component_name, and aliases. Results match if any of these
fields contain the query string. Can be combined with other filters.
parameters.name: query

get.description: GetAdvisory retrieves a single advisory by UID.

parameters.description: UID of the advisory to retrieve.
parameters.name: uid
